summary: Tested GymNite end-to-end: backend auth/receipt/admin APIs (pytest, 17/18 passing) and frontend E2E (landing, register->dashboard, JPG upload -> Pendiente, admin login, approve). Found one critical bug: admin user list returns has_receipt=false for every user, so the 'Ver' (view receipt) button never appears in the admin table, blocking the receipt-modal approval flow.

critical.issue: Always returns has_receipt=false even when the user uploaded a receipt. Root cause: list_users() Mongo projection in server.py (line 213) is {"_id":0,"password_hash":0,"receipt_image":0} — it excludes receipt_image, then serialize_user() evaluates bool(u.get('receipt_image')) -> False unconditionally. As a result the admin UI shows '—' instead of 'Ver' for every user and cannot open the receipt modal.

minor.issue: Cookies are issued with SameSite=None; Secure=True hardcoded. Works on the HTTPS preview URL but will silently fail on http://localhost. Acceptable for the preview, flagging for awareness.
allow_origins falls back to http://localhost:3000 — fine but the FRONTEND_URL env is the source of truth; consider removing the fallback to fail fast on misconfig.

ui_bugs.issue: All rows show '—' in COMPROBANTE column even after a user uploads a JPG, so the receipt modal (data-testid='receipt-modal') is never reachable from the UI. Direct consequence of the /api/admin/users projection bug above.

integration_issues.issue: View receipt button is not rendered because backend reports has_receipt=false. Approving directly from the row still works (verified manually in first E2E run, status flipped to Suscrito).

action_items: Fix /api/admin/users projection in /app/backend/server.py:213. Simplest fix: remove 'receipt_image': 0 from the projection (small admin list, base64 size is acceptable) OR use a Mongo aggregation that adds {has_receipt: {$cond: [{$ifNull: ['$receipt_image', false]}, true, false]}} and excludes receipt_image. After fix the admin UI 'Ver' button will appear and the modal-approve flow will work end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py list_users() projection bug (excludes field then asks bool() of it) — root cause of the critical issue above.
ADMIN_PASSWORD=12345 hardcoded in .env is acceptable for the demo but should be rotated before any non-preview deployment.

Receipt is stored as base64 directly inside the user document. Fine for MVP but limits future scaling (Mongo doc size limit / list endpoints become heavy if projection bug is fixed by simply removing the exclusion).
server.py is approaching 320 lines and mixes auth, receipts, admin and seed in one file. Still fine but split into routers if it grows.
Cookies issued unconditionally with Secure=True and SameSite=None — local http testing would fail; document this explicitly.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (landing + register + dashboard + upload + admin login + row-level approve all working; receipt modal flow blocked by backend bug)

test_credentials: Admin admin@gymnite.com / 12345 (auto-seeded, verified). Test users generated dynamically via /api/auth/register with TEST_/test_e2e_ prefixes.
seed_data_creation: Created several test users via /api/auth/register during testing (TEST_user_*, TEST_logout_*, TEST_noreceipt_*, test_e2e_*). They have UUID ids and live alongside the seeded admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the projection bug in list_users(), re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_gymnite.py (the test_admin_users_list assertion will pass once has_receipt is true). Then re-run the frontend admin E2E to confirm the 'Ver' button appears, the receipt modal opens with a data:image/jpeg base64 src, and modal-approve-btn flips the row badge to Suscrito.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: 1) Register user; 2) POST /api/receipts/upload with a JPG; 3) GET /api/admin/users as admin -> the user object has has_receipt=false even though /api/admin/users/{id}/receipt returns the saved image correctly. Cause: db.users.find(..., {"_id":0,"password_hash":0,"receipt_image":0}) strips receipt_image, then serialize_user() builds has_receipt = bool(u.get('receipt_image')) which is bool(None) = False for every user. Mitigation: include receipt_image in the projection and let serialize_user compute the boolean, or pre-compute has_receipt server-side via aggregation and continue excluding the heavy field.
